title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the image, a rectangular region contains a uniform, steady magnetic field. A semicircular closed wire loop rotates counterclockwise with constant angular velocity about point O in the plane of the paper. Point O is the center of the semicircle and lies exactly at the edge of the magnetic-field region. Time starts when the semicircular loop is completely outside the magnetic field. Which graph label corresponds to the induced electromotive force versus time for the semicircular wire loop?

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: In the Op-Amp circuit shown in the image, assume that the diode current follows the equation \( I = I_s e^{(V / V_T)} \). For \( V_i = 2V \), let \( V_o = V_{o1} \), and for \( V_i = 4V \), let \( V_o = V_{o2} \). What is the relationship between \( V_{o1} \) and \( V_{o2} \)?

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text:
The figure shows the single line diagram of a single machine infinite bus system.

The inertia constant of the synchronous generator \( H = 5 \ \text{MW-s/MVA} \). Frequency is 50 Hz. Mechanical power is 1 pu. The system is operating at the stable equilibrium point with rotor angle \( \delta \) equal to \( 30^\circ \). A three-phase short circuit fault occurs at a certain location on one of the circuits of the double circuit transmission line. During fault, electrical power in pu is \( P_{\text{max}} \sin \delta \). If the values of \( \delta \) and \( \frac{d\delta}{dt} \) at the instant of fault clearing are \( 45^\circ \) and 3.762 radian/s respectively, what is \( P_{\text{max}} \) in pu?
